topic.what: Systematic risk assessment framework combining HARA and TARA to identify and analyze safety and security risks from DNN limitations in autonomous driving perception
topic.how: Proposes joint HARA-TARA workflow applying ISO 26262 and ISO/SAE 21434 methodologies to five DNN limitations: lack of generalization, efficiency, explainability, plausibility, and robustness
topic.why_matters: DNN limitations in SAE L3-4 autonomous vehicles pose unaddressed cross-domain risks; existing standards do not systematically cover DNN-rooted hazards and threats in perception

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of DNN limitations systematically assessed
characteristics.evidence: Section 4 evaluates workflow on five DNN limitations (generalization, efficiency, explainability, plausibility, robustness)
characteristics.context: camera-based perception in modular AD pipeline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of security assets considered in TARA
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 defines three assets: sensor data integrity, DNN behavior, perception outputs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of automotive/AI safety and security standards analyzed
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 reviews ISO 26262, ISO/IEC TR 24028, ISO/SAE 21434, ISO 21448, ISO PAS 8800, ANSI/UL 4600, ISO/IEC TR 24029, ISO/IEC TR 5469, EU AI Act
characteristics.context: Coverage of DNN limitations in existing standards

characteristics.what_it_measures: ASIL levels assigned to identified hazards
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 assigns ASIL A-D based on severity, exposure, controllability; robustness and generalization yield ASIL D
characteristics.context: Subjective assignment based on authors judgment per paper acknowledgment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Risk treatment decisions for threat scenarios
characteristics.evidence: Table 4 assigns risk levels and treatment decisions (risk reduction, acceptance, monitoring)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of safety and security mitigation strategies identified
characteristics.evidence: Tables 3-4 propose mitigations: data augmentation, OOD detection, robustness testing, XAI, adversarial training, sensor fusion, plausibility constraints

requires: subject matter expertise for risk scoring

novelty: Joint HARA-TARA workflow for DNN limitations
DNN insufficiency mapping to ASIL and threat scenarios
Standards coverage gap analysis for DNN limitations
open_problems: Subjective risk scoring without empirical incident data
